title: 18MB compiled binary go/acs/cycle536/evolve is TRACKED in git and was re-committed grown to 18.4MB in ship 0405658a - remove it, gitignore compiled binaries under go/acs/**, and add a commit-time guard so no cycle ships a binary artifact again
summary: Spotted 2026-07-07 in ship 0405658a's stat: 'go/acs/cycle536/evolve | Bin 18397266 -> 18467058 bytes'. A cycle-536-era test apparently committed a compiled evolve binary into its ACS dir; some suite rebuilds it and cycle ships keep re-committing the changed blob (~18MB per version in git history - repo bloat + a stale binary that could be mistaken for an authorized one; the ship-sha gate pins go/evolve, not this stray).
root_cause: An ACS suite built a binary into its own package dir (instead of t.TempDir or -o /dev/null) and the cycle ship's stage-everything committed it; .gitignore covers go/bin/ and go/evolve patterns but not go/acs/**/evolve.
fix: (1) Find the acs/cycle536 test that builds it; redirect output to t.TempDir(). (2) git rm --cached go/acs/cycle536/evolve + .gitignore entry for compiled binaries under go/acs/ (and any stray 'evolve' executable outside go/bin). (3) Guard: extend the secret-leak-scan or commit path with a binary-artifact check (size>1MB + executable + Mach-O/ELF magic) that blocks staging - fail loudly. Do NOT rewrite history (the old blobs stay; P0 rule - no history rewrites).
acceptance: go/acs/cycle536/evolve untracked + ignored; the suite that built it uses a temp path and still passes.
A staged >1MB executable fails the guard with an actionable message (regression test).
connects_to: loop-binary-drift-self-sha (stray binaries near the authorized one)
notes: Weight 0.82: real repo bloat + integrity hygiene, below the width/coverage work.
